last_user_message:
## Role
You are the BUILDER for Phase 2 of Vibe-Auto-Cost. You build code and self-test. You do NOT self-evaluate against the spec (a separate evaluator does that).

## User Goal (verbatim)
> Execute Phase 2 implementation, building the final web application completely inside the dedicated directory './auto-evaluation/app/'. Use the cloned interface in './autodoc-clone' as your base layout and asset reference. You must strictly read, ingest, and adhere to the architectural rules defined in './auto-evaluation/logic_guide_v1.md' for backend engineering, and './auto-evaluation/visual_architecture_101.md' for all UI injection and chart styles.
> Core Execution Plan: (1) Data Pipeline â€” extract the data vault archives and run the Heuristic Alignment Matrix to merge the datasets. (2) Backend Logic â€” deterministic 5-year cost engine with strict Pydantic JSON schema validation, wired to serve the frontend. (3) Frontend UI â€” dashboard injected below the Autodoc vehicle selector, using official Autodoc CSS design tokens. (4) Visualization â€” Chart.js or ApexCharts via CDN: cumulative 5-year cost splits + geometric Reliability Radar. (5) Verification â€” adversary evaluation worker runs visual/functional validation; fix layout shifts, unstyled backgrounds, path errors.

## FIRST â€” investigate (do NOT code yet)
Read these files IN FULL before proposing anything:
1. `/workspace/auto-evaluation/logic_guide_v1.md` â€” backend spec (data pipeline, alignment matrix, cost formula, JSON schema, guardrails).
2. `/workspace/auto-evaluation/visual_architecture_101.md` â€” UI spec (color tokens, injection strategy, chart styling, evaluator snippet).
3. `/root/.adal/engineer/contracts/2026-07-phase2-contract.md` â€” the full contract (my synthesis + ground-truth schema + architecture decision). READ THIS â€” it has the REAL dataset schema and decided architecture.
4. The extracted data: `/workspace/auto-evaluation/data/extracted_uci/car.data` (head it) and inspect the xlsx schema (already done by supervisor â€” see contract Â§"European dataset schema").
5. The Phase-1 clone to reuse as the selector base: `/workspace/autodoc-clone/src/components/` and `/workspace/autodoc-clone/dist/` (the built version).

## Ground truth (verified by supervisor â€” trust over the guide's assumptions)
- Workspace root = `/workspace/auto-evaluation/`. App target = `/workspace/auto-evaluation/app/`.
- Data is ALREADY extracted to `./data/extracted_european/EUROPEAN CARS DATASET.xlsx` (6000 rows Ă— 25 cols) and `./data/extracted_uci/car.data` (1728 rows). You do NOT need to unzip again, but DO write the extraction step into a `./app/backend/load_data.py` so it's reproducible (re-extracts if missing).
- The guide assumed CSV; it's actually **xlsx**. Use `openpyxl` (installed). Real columns listed in the contract.
- Python 3.11 + pydantic 2.13 + fastapi + uvicorn + openpyxl are installed system-wide (no venv needed; `python3` on PATH).
- Phase-1 clone is React+Vite+Tailwind. For Phase 2 you do NOT need to rebuild the selector in React â€” you can copy the Phase-1 `dist/` build into `./app/frontend/` and inject the dashboard as an additional HTML/JS module, OR rebuild a single self-contained `index.html` that reuses the selector's markup + assets + tokens. Choose the simpler, more robust path. The dashboard must appear BELOW the selector with id `#evaluation-dashboard-root`.

## Architecture (decided by supervisor â€” implement this)
- **Backend:** FastAPI at `./app/backend/main.py`. Endpoint `GET /api/evaluate?make=&model=&engine=` returns the EXACT JSON schema from logic_guide Â§4. Pydantic models (`ReliabilityScores`, `FinancialProjections`, `EvaluateResponse`) enforce validation. Also serve the frontend static files from FastAPI (mount `./app/frontend/`) so the whole app runs on one port (simpler, no CORS). 
- **Data engine:** `./app/backend/load_data.py` loads xlsx + car.data at startup into an in-memory index keyed by (Brand, model-stripped). Implements the Heuristic Alignment Matrix (logic_guide Â§3): makeâ†’buying tier (premium=vhigh/high, volume=med/low), engineâ†’maint tier (>2.0L or EV = vhigh/high, â‰¤1.4L or hybrid = low/med). Fallback chain: exact model â†’ strip sub-model badges â†’ match make + closest engine â†’ global median.
- **Cost formula** (logic_guide Â§4): annual_fuel = `Energy Cost (â‚¬/100km)` Ă— 150 (15000km). For EV: `Efficiency (Wh/km) Ă— 15000 / 1000 Ă— 0.30 â‚¬/kWh`. annual_maintenance = UCI maint mapped (vhigh=1200, high=850, med=500, low=250). total_5yr = (fuel+maint)Ă—5. Guardrails: â‰¤0 â†’ HTTP 422 exception; vibe_score clamp [0,100]; >â‚¬150000 â†’ cap at 150000 + add `"flagged": true` to metadata.
- **Frontend:** `./app/frontend/index.html` (self-contained, served by FastAPI). Top = the autodoc vehicle selector (reuse Phase-1 markup + CSS tokens + fonts + logo + the make/model/engine dropdown data). Bottom = `#evaluation-dashboard-root` (initially a "Waiting for selection" placeholder in dark token style). On Buscar/Evaluate click â†’ fetch `/api/evaluate` â†’ dashboard animates open (height/opacity transition) â†’ render two Chart.js charts.
- **Charts (Chart.js via CDN):**
  - Chart 1: 5-year cumulative cost â€” grouped/stacked bars, x-axis Year 1â€“5, fuel series `#ffd800`, maintenance series `#ff8a00`, transparent bg.
  - Chart 2: reliability radar â€” axes [Safety, Maintenance(inverted), Boot Capacity, Comfort], fill opacity 0.2 brand color, grid `#334155`.
- **Visual tokens (visual_architecture Â§1 â€” MANDATORY for the dashboard):** `--autodoc-dark-bg:#22252a; --autodoc-card-bg:#1b1d22; --autodoc-brand-yellow:#ffd800; --autodoc-brand-orange:#ff8a00; --autodoc-text-main:#ffffff; --autodoc-text-muted:#94a3b8; --autodoc-border-gray:#334155;`. Hover transitions `all 0.2s ease-in-out` grayâ†’orange. Dropdowns 4px radius, yellow active outline. Dashboard bg must NOT be white/transparent (evaluator asserts this).

## CRITICAL evaluator assertion (visual_architecture Â§4) â€” your dashboard MUST pass this
```js
const selectorBox = document.querySelector('.search-form, .selector-wrapper, #car-selection-container');
const dashboardBox = document.querySelector('#evaluation-dashboard-root');
// dashboardRect.top must be >= selectorRect.bottom (dashboard BELOW selector)
// dashboardBox backgroundColor must NOT be rgb(255,255,255) or rgba(0,0,0,0)
```
So: give the selector a recognizable class/id (e.g. `#car-selection-container`), give the dashboard id `#evaluation-dashboard-root`, and set an explicit dark background on it.

## Phase A â€” Propose (write a short plan, then implement)
After reading, write `/workspace/auto-evaluation/app/BUILD_PLAN.md` with: file structure, the exact Pydantic schema, the alignment-matrix pseudocode, the frontend injection approach (how you reuse Phase-1 selector + add dashboard), chart config, and a list of assets to copy from `/workspace/autodoc-clone/`. Then proceed to implement â€” you do NOT need to wait for my approval on the plan (this is a build sprint); implement to the contract.

## Phase B â€” Implement
Build everything under `/workspace/auto-evaluation/app/`:
- `backend/main.py` (FastAPI app, Pydantic schema, `/api/evaluate`, static mount, CORS off since same-origin).
- `backend/load_data.py` (xlsx + car.data loader, alignment matrix, fallback resolver, cost calculator, guardrails).
- `backend/__init__.py`.
- `frontend/index.html` (selector + dashboard shell), `frontend/app.js` (fetch + chart rendering), `frontend/styles.css` (dashboard dark tokens; reuse Phase-1 selector CSS).
- Copy needed assets (fonts, logo, banner) from `/workspace/autodoc-clone/public/` into `./app/frontend/assets/`.
- `requirements.txt` (pydantic, fastapi, uvicorn[standard], openpyxl, python-multipart).
- `README.md` (how to run: `cd app/backend && uvicorn main:app --port 8000` â†’ open http://127.0.0.1:8000).

## Phase C â€” Self-test (you MUST pass before reporting)
1. `cd /workspace/auto-evaluation/app/backend && python3 -c "import main; print('import ok')"` â†’ clean.
2. Start the server: `cd /workspace/auto-evaluation/app/backend && (uvicorn main:app --port 8000 > /tmp/p2-server.log 2>&1 &) && sleep 4`.
3. `curl -s 'http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/evaluate?make=BMW&model=1%20Series&engine=Petrol' | python3 -m json.tool` â†’ 200 + schema-valid JSON with all required fields.
4. Test the guardrails: a make that doesn't exist should still return a valid fallback (global median), NOT a 500. A â‰¤0 path should 422.
5. Fetch the root: `curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://127.0.0.1:8000/` â†’ 200.
6. Take a Playwright screenshot of the full app at 1440Ă—900: load http://127.0.0.1:8000/, select a make/model/engine, click evaluate, wait for charts, screenshot â†’ save to `/workspace/auto-evaluation/app/screenshot.png`. Verify the dashboard appears below the selector with dark bg and both charts render. (Playwright is at `/app/node_modules/playwright`; run scripts with `NODE_PATH=/app/node_modules node ...`.)
7. Run the evaluator snippet from visual_architecture Â§4 in the browser via Playwright â€” confirm it returns `{passed: true}`.

## Report back
- Files created (tree of `./app/`).
- `python3 -c "import main"` result.
- The curl JSON output for a sample query (proving the schema + guardrails).
- Confirmation the Playwright screenshot shows selector-on-top + dark dashboard below + 2 charts.
- The evaluator-snippet result.
- Any deviations from the contract and why.
Do NOT write an eval report. The evaluator worker (separate) runs after. Report 'built + self-tests pass' and stop.

## Rules
- Self-contained in `./app/`. Copy assets in; don't reference `/workspace/autodoc-clone` at runtime.
- Strictly follow logic_guide Â§4 schema + guardrails and visual_architecture Â§1 tokens.
- Surgical: don't over-engineer. Deterministic calculations only.
- If the xlsx has a column the guide's formula needs but names differently, adapt sensibly and note it in BUILD_PLAN.md.
- Keep the selector's Spanish labels (Phase-1 fidelity) where they exist.

Begin by reading the two guides + the contract + car.data, then write BUILD_PLAN.md, then implement.
